concerns.text: T006 mandates loading MiniMax-M3 with 'NO quantization' via `transformers`. This directly violates the Plan.md constraint ('MUST use GGUF low-bit quantization') and the Constitution's feasibility requirement (SC-005) to fit the model in 7GB RAM. The task deliverable will fail to run on the target runner.

T020 implements a 'Wilcoxon signed-rank test'. The Plan.md Summary and Constitution Check explicitly mandate a 'TOST equivalence test' to verify the heuristic is within the ±2% tolerance (SC-001/SC-002). Wilcoxon tests for difference, not equivalence; this semantic drift invalidates the statistical validation of the core research claim.

T004 downloads RULER data and computes checksums but omits the task to record the SHA-256 hash in `state/projects/PROJ-937-llmxive-follow-up-extending-minimax-spar.yaml`. This violates Constitution Principle III (Data Hygiene) which requires the checksum to be recorded in the state file for reproducibility.

T024-T026 implement sensitivity analysis but omit the specific 'high sensitivity' warning logic defined in Spec.md US-3 (flag if accuracy drop >5% for a 10-block increase). The task description mentions a 'notable threshold' but fails to enforce the specific 5% acceptance criterion, risking incomplete validation of SC-004.

T014 logs Exact Match scores but does not explicitly include the task to compute and log 'heuristic_time' vs 'inference_time' separately. This violates Constitution Principle VI (Zero-Parameter Heuristic Fidelity) and SC-003, which require isolating routing overhead from model inference to validate the efficiency claim.
